body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

The spec describes a model where the parent uses a SendMessage-style tool to dispatch work to a previously-spawned background child that continues independently after the parent's turn ends. Two key invariants distinguish it from foreground-subagent: (1) the parent goes `working → ready` at turn end even though the child is still alive, and (2) `SubagentCount(parent)` excludes the background child.

Claude Code's current Agent tool is foreground-by-default. The CLI does not (as of the version this adapter targets) expose a stable user-facing 'send message to background subagent' surface — the Task tool blocks the parent's turn until the subagent reports back. Subagent transcripts under `<parent>/subagents/` are written for every Agent invocation, but the parent-wait semantics are baked into the tool's design. Without a SendMessage-like primitive, the scenario's distinguishing observable (parent goes ready while child stays alive) cannot be produced from the CLI.

irrlicht's transport would, however, observe a background subagent IF Claude Code emitted one: the fswatcher already discovers the sidechain transcript independently of the parent's turn arc, and the detector already evaluates each session's state on its own metrics. The missing piece is purely upstream — the parent transcript never emits the 'fire and forget' Agent variant. If/when Anthropic ships a true background-task primitive (similar to OpenCode's `run_in_background` or the proposed Claude background-jobs feature), the existing file-based path would already do the right thing: parent's turn would close at `end_turn`, child transcript would continue ticking, and `SubagentCount(parent)` derived from live linked children would still reflect the running background child.

A related but distinct surface — `Bash run_in_background: true` for shell processes — IS supported by Claude Code, but that's `background-process` (no JSONL transcript, no SessionState), not a subagent. They are separately assessed.

Net: agent_supports is `partial` because the Claude Code TUI does not currently expose a way to produce the scenario's defining shape (parent ready while named child still works in the same lineage). irrlicht_observes is `partial` because the discovery and parent-linkage path would work IF the upstream signal existed, but `SubagentCount(parent)` does not currently distinguish background from foreground children — every linked child counts equally. A future fix would mark background-child SessionStates with a flag the metrics layer subtracts from the parent's SubagentCount.
caveats: If Claude Code ships a future background-task primitive (analogous to OpenAI's cloud Codex background agents, or a `nohup`-like extension of the Agent tool), the agent-level half of this verdict could move to `yes` without code changes — discovery is already file-based.

The metrics-layer distinction (excluding background children from SubagentCount(parent)) is not implemented — currently every parent_linked child counts. A future enhancement would tag the child's SessionState with a background-flag (e.g. from a sidechain transcript field) so the count reflects only foreground children. Issue should be filed if/when upstream supports the feature.
Cloud background agents (Anthropic's hosted long-running agents) are a separate scenario (`cloud-background-agent`) and not the same surface as this one.

sources.note: CountOpenSubagents (line 553) returns 0 — file-based path is single source of truth. No current distinction between foreground and background linked children.
FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir()} — fswatcher would discover a background subagent transcript identically to a foreground one. The agent-level gap is upstream.
Spec assumes a SendMessage-style primitive that lets the parent go ready while a named child continues. Claude Code's Task tool today blocks the parent until the subagent reports back.
